message:
chore: bump to 0.1.42, drop fastmcp-rust git patch (0.2.1 on crates.io)

- fastmcp-rust v0.2.1 is now published to crates.io along with all 8
  of its sibling crates, so the [patch.crates-io] git override is no
  longer needed.  `cargo install --git` and plain `cargo install
  beads_rust` both now resolve every dependency from crates.io with no
  sibling checkouts required (issue #250 bug 1).
- Cargo.toml: bump fastmcp-rust dep to "0.2.1" and remove the entire
  [patch.crates-io] table.
- Cargo.lock: regenerate; every `source = ...` line is now
  `registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index`.
- release.yml: remove the frankensqlite / asupersync sibling-clone
  steps from both build-release and update-crates-io jobs (dead code
  without the patch) and drop `continue-on-error: true` from the
  crates.io publish step so future token/auth failures fail loudly
  instead of silently leaving the crate behind.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -105,12 +105,6 @@ jobs:
         with:
           fetch-depth: 0  # Full history for vergen
 
-      - name: Checkout frankensqlite (path dependency)
-        run: git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite.git ../frankensqlite
-
-      - name: Checkout asupersync (path dependency of fsqlite-core)
-        run: git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync.git ../asupersync
-
       - name: Install Rust toolchain
         uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
         with:
@@ -455,17 +449,10 @@ jobs:
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683  # v4.2.2
 
-      - name: Checkout frankensqlite (path dependency)
-        run: git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite.git ../frankensqlite
-
-      - name: Checkout asupersync (path dependency of fsqlite-core)
-        run: git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync.git ../asupersync
-
       - name: Install Rust toolchain
         uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
         with:
           toolchain: nightly
 
       - name: Publish to crates.io
         run: cargo publish --token ${{ secrets.CRATES_IO_TOKEN }}
-        continue-on-error: true  # Don't fail release if crates.io publish fails

+version = "0.1.42"
 edition = "2024"
 rust-version = "1.88"
 description = "Agent-first issue tracker (SQLite + JSONL)"
@@ -106,7 +106,7 @@ unicode-width = "0.2.2"
 semver = "1.0.27"
 
 # MCP server (optional; gated behind the `mcp` feature)
-fastmcp-rust = { version = "0.2.0", optional = true }
+fastmcp-rust = { version = "0.2.1", optional = true }
 
 # Self-update (optional; gated behind the `self_update` feature)
 self_update = { version = "0.42", features = ["rustls", "archive-tar", "compression-flate2"], default-features = false, optional = true }
@@ -152,21 +152,17 @@ self_update = [
 
 # NOTE: no `[patch.crates-io]` here.
 #
-# fsqlite 0.1.2+ and all fsqlite-* transitive crates are published on
-# crates.io, so this crate resolves its dependencies from there out of the
-# box.  That makes `cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust.git`
-# work without requiring a sibling `frankensqlite` checkout (issue #250 bug 1).
+# All dependencies — fsqlite 0.1.2+, its fsqlite-* transitive crates, and
+# fastmcp-rust 0.2.1+ — are published on crates.io, so this crate
+# resolves everything from the registry out of the box.  That makes
+# `cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust.git`
+# work without requiring sibling `frankensqlite` or `fastmcp_rust`
+# checkouts (issue #250 bug 1).
 #
-# Local frankensqlite co-development: contributors who want to hack on
-# frankensqlite alongside beads_rust can add a workspace-level
+# Local co-development: contributors who want to hack on frankensqlite
+# or fastmcp_rust alongside beads_rust can add a workspace-level
 # `.cargo/config.toml` with their own `[patch.crates-io]` table.  See
 # `scripts/dev-local-frankensqlite.toml` for a ready-to-copy template.
-#
-# The `fastmcp-rust` crate is still unpublished, so keep its git override
-# here — this is the only patch we need to ship with the crate, and it
-# does not reference any local paths.
-[patch.crates-io]
-fastmcp-rust         = { git = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/fastmcp_rust", package = "fastmcp-rust" }
 
 [lints.rust]
 unsafe_code = "forbid"
